title: From Plan to Action: Workforce Convening Centers on Coordinated Training Strategies
summary: HOME | Page 20 From Plan to Action: Workforce Convening Centers on Coordinated Training Strategies Photo Credit: Alaska Works Partnership What’s a plan without action? Alaska’s Workforce Future , a document released in March by the Alaska Department of Labor and Workforce Development (DOLWD) and the Alaska Workforce Alliance (AWA), reflects extensive collaboration, with feedback from industry representatives, job seekers, educators, and workforce partners. To advance the plan’s proactive approach to meeting employment demand, DOLWD and several partners will host a...

title: AST DWSOL: Glennallen [AK25101511]
summary: On 10/05/2025 at approximately 1457 hours, a Trooper conducted a traffic stop for equipment and moving violations. The driver, Jeremy Mishler, had a revoked license and multiple prior driving while revoked convictions within the last ten years. Charges are being forwarded for driving while revoked.

title: AST Run Away Juveniles: Fairbanks [AK25101326]
summary: On 10/05/2025 at approximately 130 hours 2 juvenile females were placed into run away status after they ran away from a juvenile care facility in Fairbanks. Troopers are actively searching for them and have distributed information out to the proper outlets to aid in the search.

title: Dan Schultz: 2025 Haines Borough Planning Commission candidate, three-year seat
summary: Incumbent Dan Schultz wants to serve another term on the Haines Borough Planning Commission. He says he puts the time into understanding borough code and preparing for Planning meetings. KHNS reporter Melinda Munson spoke with Shultz at the Haines studio. Schultz: “I’ve been up here for about 23 yea...

title: Rachel Saitzyk: 2025 Haines Borough Planning Commission candidate, three-year seat
summary: Incumbent Rachel Saitzyk wants to serve another term on the Haines Borough Planning Commission. She says her ability to connect and her optimism make her a good fit for the position. KHNS’ Melinda Munson met with the candidate to find out more. Saitzyk: “I’m a 39-year-old small business owner … I ar...

title: Kimberly Rosado: 2025 Haines Borough Planning Commission candidate, three-year term
summary: Kimberly Rosado almost always gives citizen comment at Haines Borough Assembly meetings. She’s switching her focus, and hopes to be elected to the Planning Commission. title: Nathanael Reams: 2025 Haines Brough Planning Commission candidate, three-year seat
summary: Nathanael Reams is running for the Haines Planning Commission. He says he’s entering the race as a new candidate, willing to learn and work with other commissioners. KHNS’ Melinda Munson interviewed Reams over the phone from his work site on the north side of Kodiak Island. Reams: “I grew up all ove...

title: Joe Parnell: 2025 Haines Borough Planning Commission candidate, three-year seat
summary: Joe Parnell has held various jobs. He’s hoping to add planning commissioner to his resume. KHNS reporter Melinda Munson visited with the candidate, who says he particulalry concerned about Haines’ derelict buildings. Parnell: “Well, I’m 63. No criminal record. title: AST DUI, MIW4: Wasilla [AK25101360]
summary: On 10/05/2025 at approximately 0015 hours, while investigating a separate incident, Alaska State Troopers observed a black Chevrolet pickup truck drive past multiple marked Trooper vehicles in an erratic manner. Troopers conducted a traffic stop on the vehicle after observing additional driving violations. Following an investigation, Isiah Davis, age 23 of Palmer, was arrested and remanded to Mat-Su Pretrial for driving under the influence of alcohol and misconduct involving a weapon in the fourth degree, where he was held on an OR bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

subSources.title: AST Warrant Arrest: Tok [AK25095675]
subSources.summary: On 10/4/2025 at approximately 1700 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Tok contacted and arrested Justin West, 34, of Tok. Justin was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center on his Warrant for charges of Assault 2, Terroristic Threatening, Coercion and Assault 4.
